TextENGNew: ...I don't know how many times I've regretted my actions since then.
I would always come back to my senses after doing something I could never take back... and fall into intense self-loathing that drove me to want to destroy myself... And the cycle never ended.
Let this be the last time.
From now on, I'll repent and straighten things out.
I said that over and over... so many times that I was disgusted.
In the end, I'll always get triggered by the most truly insignificant things... and I'll do it anyway.
When we started dating, I was supposed to pull her closer.
She was a little indecisive and shy, but... her gentle smile was so cute. I swore to myself that I'd make her the happiest person in the world, with my own two hands.
Before I knew it, the overflowing talent I thought I was blessed with slowly dried up. When I noticed how much my rusty arms squeaked as I moved them... I understood.
I lacked the strength to do it.
And I even lacked the right to do it.
But despite all that, she was always smiling and watching over me...
No matter how useless I became, she always surrounded me in the same tenderness...
No, that's a lie.
The truth is, even she should realize it sooner or later.
That even if we're together, that's not enough to make her happy.
And I don't have the power to make it reality...
So... in that case, what should I do?
Apologize to her?
Abandon my aspirations up until this point and let her down... yet ask her to stay with me regardless?
...I can't do that.
There's no way I could.
If the truth of my situation were exposed, there's no way she could continue to love me, being the pathetic dog I am.
She fell in love with me because I was talented and brimming with confidence.
So, how could she ever be attracted to a weak, talentless man like me?
Now that both of those things are gone, wouldn't it be impossible for the feelings in her heart to go on?
I was afraid.
I was afraid of her looking down on me, giving up on me... and abandoning me.
That's why.
Even though I'd resolved myself to make things easier for her and finally obtained this after shedding blood and tears... I couldn't show it to her.
If I show her that, maybe she'll be delighted.
At the very least, I won't be lazing about like I am right now, so that should make life a little easier.
That way, the weight on her back can get a little lighter, and maybe she'll be able to smile again.
But... what about me?
Is it possible for her to continue loving me?
After all, I've already demonstrated myself as a completely mediocre guy... Can we really keep the relationship going like before?
The beer tasted awful.
But feeling drunk might help me take that step anyway.
Even if I keep drinking like I have been these days... I still can't bring myself to gather the courage to show her... I'm terrified by the prospect of losing her love...
